div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in Japan (Grew)
div.p: 35. Department’s instructions No. 639, November 19, 1934. We would appreciate receipt as soon as practicable of expression of your views.The Ambassador’s reply, telegram No. 51, March 9, 1 p.m., suggested “delay pending Russo-Japanese fishery discussions.” (894.628/121)

An official of the Bureau of Marine Products, Japanese Department of Agriculture and Forestry, who is now in the United States, manifested to an officer of the Department and to an officer of the Bureau of Fisheries interest in the possibility of revision of the Fur Seals Treaty.Convention between the United States, the United Kingdom, Japan, and Russia for the preservation and protection of fur seals, signed July 7, 1911, Foreign Relations, 1911, p. 260. Have there been indications of a revival of interest in Japan in this matter?For previous correspondence, see Foreign Relations, 1928, vol. iii, pp. 147 ff.
Please refrain from consulting Japanese officials in connection with either of these matters.
